## Executive Summary
The retrieved documents offer some investigative findings regarding the HSCA's examination of Jack Ruby, including the HSCA's investigation and the release of related records [1]. The documents also mention the HSCA's sequestered collection, which includes documents related to Jack Ruby [1]. However, the records do not provide a comprehensive overview of the HSCA's examination of Jack Ruby.

## Detailed Findings
### HSCA Investigation
The HSCA conducted an investigation into the assassination of President Kennedy, which included an examination of Jack Ruby [1]. The investigation resulted in the creation of a sequestered collection, which includes documents related to Jack Ruby [1]. The documents also mention that the HSCA brought together in one place the principal documents retrieved over the course of years from earlier investigations, including the Warren Commission, the Rockefeller Commission, and the Church Committee [1].

### Release of Records
The Review Board has made public some of the records related to the HSCA's investigation, including thousands of CIA documents on Lee Harvey Oswald and the assassination of President Kennedy [3]. The documents also mention that the Review Board has released thousands of once-secret records from the investigation by the House Select Committee on Assassinations, including the controversial Staff Report on Oswald's trip to Mexico City [3]. However, the records do not specifically mention the release of records related to Jack Ruby.

### HSCA Sequestered Collection
The HSCA sequestered collection includes documents related to Jack Ruby, as well as other individuals and organizations [1]. The collection is described as containing over 300,000 pages of documents, including duplicates [1]. The documents also mention that the collection was created by the HSCA and was initially sealed, but has since been made available to the public [1].

### Mention of Jack Ruby
Jack Ruby is mentioned in several documents, including a list of HSCA names [5] and a memo from the Center for CIA Security [2]. The documents also mention that the Review Board has identified and acquired significant assassination-related records, including the original personal papers of Clay Shaw, who was prosecuted in New Orleans in 1969 for the assassination of President Kennedy [4]. However, the records do not provide a detailed examination of Jack Ruby's role in the assassination or the HSCA's investigation of him.
